thinking.text:
Let me analyze this carefully. 

First, the account was opened on 11/10/2022.

The current date is 11/14/2025.

The monthly windows for the Platinum Rewards Card start on the account opening date (the 10th of each month, based on 11/10).

So monthly windows are from the 10th of one month to the 9th of the next month (or the day before the next monthly anniversary).

Now regarding the annual fee and rebate:

The knowledge base says:
- The annual fee rebate is earned only if you meet the required monthly spending threshold of $7,500 in every month of your cardmember year.
- If your first cardmember year's annual fee is waived under a promotional offer, the rebate does not apply to that year.

Let me check the transactions to see if there's an annual fee being charged. Looking at all the transactions, I only see purchase transactions, no annual fee charges. So it's a bit unclear whether the first year was waived or not.

Actually, there's no information about whether the first year's fee was waived. Let me think about what I can tell the user based on available data.

Let me look at the cardmember year that just ended. Since we're at 11/14/2025:

Cardmember years based on account opening of 11/10/2022:
- Year 1: 11/10/2022 - 11/09/2023
- Year 2: 11/10/2023 - 11/09/2024
- Year 3: 11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025
- Year 4: 11/10/2025 - 11/09/2026 (current)

The most recently completed year is Year 3 (11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025).

Let me check the transactions for Year 3. Monthly windows from 10th to 9th:

Month 1 (Year 3): 11/10/2024 - 12/09/2024
Transactions in this window:
- 11/10/2024: $424.45 (New Seasons Market)
- 11/12/2024: $2,126.65 (McMenamins)
- 11/20/2024: $504.92 (Powell's Books)
- 11/24/2024: $4,179.24 (New Seasons Market)
- 12/03/2024: $358.92 (Pok Pok Restaurant)

Total: $424.45 + $2,126.65 + $504.92 + $4,179.24 + $358.92 = $7,594.18

That's above $7,500! Great.

Wow! If Year 3 is the relevant year for the rebate (assuming the first year's fee was waived or not), they actually met the $7,500 threshold in every single month.

But wait - I'm not sure if the first year was waived or not. Let me check if there are any annual fee transactions visible.

Looking at all 60 transactions, I don't see any annual fee charges. But the transactions only go from 11/10/2024 to 10/26/2025. 

Also, the user said they've "had the card for about a year" - but the account was opened on 11/10/2022 which is 3 years ago. So maybe they're mistaken about the timeline, or maybe they got it more recently? The data says 11/10/2022 though.

Let me think about this differently. Maybe this is their first cardmember year where the fee isn't waived? Let me check...

Actually, I don't have info about whether the fee was waived in Year 1. But the account was opened 11/10/2022. 

Let me just provide the user with the information I've gathered. The monthly threshold is $7,500.00 and I can see their transactions for Year 3 (11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025).

I think the most reasonable approach is to explain how the rebate works, show what data I can see, and let them know whether based on the available information they appear to qualify.

But I should be careful - I need to know which cardmember year we're talking about and whether the first year was waived. Let me search for any more info about annual fee billing.

tool_calls.Name: KB_search
tool_calls.Arguments: {"query": "Platinum Rewards Card annual fee amount waiver promotional"}

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
Great, Yuki! I've verified your identity. Let me now look into the details of your Platinum Rewards Card account and the annual fee rebate.

Let me check a few things in the knowledge base to give you accurate information.
